tipRules.tip: Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.
Johnston: Use a 6th Sense Party Minnow on the drop shot for deep smallmouth. A minnow-style bait is key.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: On calm days for deep smallmouth, cast the drop shot out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them. Adjust presentation to match conditions even though the bait stays the same.

Johnston: Targeted an 80-yard stretch of bottom in 33 feet loaded with big smallmouth. Had about three hours of fishing time per day due to the long run, but committed to the spot and made it worth it.

Johnston: Don't abandon the plan to run to Lake Ontario because of 8- to 10-foot waves if you've prepared. Even with only about three hours of fishing time per day, the quality of the spot makes it worth it.
